description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a long, straight wooden handle with a natural brown finish and a slightly bulbous end for grip. The head of the axe is made of metallic steel, with a broad, rectangular blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end. The blade is securely mounted perpendicular to the handle, with visible metal wedges or pins at the top, suggesting a traditional mounting style. The wood grain and subtle wear on the handle indicate it is designed for repeated manual use, likely for chopping wood or similar tasks. The metal blade has a matte, slightly rough finish, and the overall construction appears robust and utilitarian. No visible markings, engravings, or brand labels are present on either the handle or the blade.
description: A robust hand axe with a long wooden handle and a broad steel blade, featuring a traditional, utilitarian design. The handle is straight with a rounded end, and the blade is rectangular and securely mounted.
description_view_0: The axe is seen at an angle, highlighting the junction between the wooden handle and the steel blade, with the poll and blade edge both partially visible.
description_view_1: This side view shows the full length of the straight wooden handle and the broad, rectangular steel blade, emphasizing the axe's overall proportions.
description_view_2: The axe is viewed from the top, displaying the narrow profile of the blade and the full length of the handle, with the mounting point clearly visible.
description_view_3: A mirrored side view of the axe, showing the opposite face of the blade and handle, with the poll and blade edge both visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a rounded end, oval cross-section, no visible grip features, natural finish.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular steel blade with a single straight edge, flat poll, no beard, and no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible metal wedge at the top of the handle.
